titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Vincenzo Sparagna Papers
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 23:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In Italian, English, French, Dutch, German, and Portuguese.

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, production files, artwork, photographs, writings, press clippings, printed material, personal papers, computer media, and sound recordings by or relating to Italian journalist and editor Vincenzo Sparagna and the Italian counterculture magazine Frigidaire . Other material in the collection pertains to related periodicals such as Cannibale , Il Male , Casting , Frizzer , Il Lunedì della Repubblica , Il Nuovo Male , Tempi Supplementari , and Vomito .

bioghist.head: Vincenzo Sparagna (1946–)
bioghist.p: Vincenzo Sparagna is an Italian journalist, editor of Frigidaire , and the director of Il Nuovo Male . Founded in 1980 and circa 1979, respectively, the progressive magazines report on Italian postwar protest culture through satire, humor, performance art, activism, and politics. The editorial board of Frigidaire published a broad range of other newspapers and magazines, including Frizzer , Vomito , Il Lunedì della Repubblica , Il Nuovo Male , and many others. Sparagna is also the president and founder of Frigolandia ("Land of Frigidaire ") in Umbria, Italy, an independent community of artistic production and expression that serves as the headquarters for Frigidaire and its related ventures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, production files, artwork, photographs, writings, press clippings, printed material, personal papers, computer media, and sound recordings by or relating to Italian journalist and editor Vincenzo Sparagna and the Italian counterculture magazine Frigidaire . Material related to the publication and operation of Frigidaire includes correspondence, production files, artwork, photographs, clippings, and printed ephemera. Also present is material relating to other periodicals published by Primo Carnera editore such as Cannibale , Il Male , Casting , Frizzer , Il Lunedì della Repubblica , Il Nuovo Male , Tempi Supplementari , and Vomito . Artwork consists of original political cartoons, comics, drawings, and posters by Angese, Giorgio Carpinteri, Pablo Echaurren, Otto Gabos, Massimo Giacon, Tanino Liberatore, Massimo Mattioli, José Muñoz, Giuseppe Palumbo, Andrea Pazienza, Filippo Scòzzari, Stefano Tamburini, Gianfranco Vanni, and Vincino. Photographs include prints, negatives, and slides collected for Frigidaire and other periodicals, as well as accompanying reportage. Images depict Rome, Naples, and other Italian cities as well as Afghanistan during the Afghan-Soviet War. Photographers with work present in the collection include Mauro Baldrati, Letizia Battaglia, Pino Cimò, Antonio Carmelo Erotico, Toty Ruggieri, Riccardo Schicchi, and Savik Shuster. The collection also contains Sparagna's personal papers and correspondence with Renato Curcio, Luigi Pintor, and Oreste Scalzone.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Frigidaire , 1973–2017. II. Cannibale and Il Male , 1977–2007. III. Other Publications Related to Frigidaire , 1983–1998. IV. General Correspondence, 1964–1997. V. Photographs, 1960–2003. VI. Artwork, 1967–2007. VII. Personal Papers, 1963–2017.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p:
This collection is open for research.
Box 11 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 12 (45 rpm records): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Frigolandia S. R. L. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Vincenzo Sparagna Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Vincenzo Sparagna Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. Information in brackets is also supplied by staff.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains professional correspondence, production files, project files, press clippings, subject files, legal files, printed material, computer media, sound recordings and other papers pertaining to Frigidaire , a counterculture magazine co-founded by Sparagna in 1980. The magazine featured underground comics, photography, reportage on social issues, and articles on arts and culture. Correspondence includes letters from contributors, exchanges with political prisoners, reader responses, and submissions to the magazine. Production files consist of artwork, cover proofs, layouts, color proofs, transparencies, photographs, and notes, as well as original political cartoons, comics, and illustrations. Artists include Giorgio Carpinteri, Pablo Echaurren, Otto Gabos, Massimo Giacon, Tanino Liberatore, Massimo Mattioli, José Muñoz, Giuseppe Palumbo, Filippo Scòzzari, and Stefano Tamburini. Editorial projects consist of drafts and notes for a series of books published under the magazine's publisher, Primo Carnera editore. The series also contains events ephemera and press clippings on Frigidaire . Printed material includes volumes, magazines, newspapers, and other publications which mention or otherwise relate to Frigidaire , as well as exhibition catalogs and posters. Also present is computer media and a 45 rpm record related to Frigidaire .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine subseries: Correspondence; Production Files; Editorial Projects; Conferences, Events, and Exhibitions; Press and Publicity; Subject Files; Legal Files; Printed Material; and Computer Media and Sound Recordings.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: Photographs and reportage published in Frigidaire can also be found in Series V. Photographs.
Artwork by contributors to Frigidaire can also be found in Series VI. Artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of production files, artwork, photographs, press clippings, correspondence, and other papers generated during Sparagna's time on the editorial staff of Cannibale , an underground comic magazine founded by Stefano Tamburini in 1977, and Il Male , a satirical magazine published from 1978 to 1982. Il Male was notable for creating numerous "falsi," fake issues of newspapers such as Corriere della sera and Pravda distributed as political satire. Production material includes cover proofs, layouts, color proofs, original artwork by Angese and Vincino, and promotional posters. Also present is a 45 rpm record produced for Il Male . The series also includes correspondence and comics related to Il Nuovo Male , a revival of Il Male published by Sparagna and Vincino between 1995 to 1996.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Cannibale ; Il Male ; Il Nuovo Male ; and Sound Recordings. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains production files, artwork, contributor submissions, and press clippings related to various periodicals created by the editorial staff of Frigidaire and published by Primo Carnera editore. These periodicals include Casting , a monthly entertainment magazine published from 1993 to 1998; Frizzer , a monthly magazine edited by Sparagna from 1985 to 1986; Il Lunedì della Repubblica , a newspaper supplement published from 1990 to 1991; Tempi Supplementari , a monthly comic magazine created by Sparagna and Stefano Tamburini between 1985 and 1986; and Vomito , a quarterly "subliterary" magazine edited by Sparagna from 1985 to 1987. Included are cover proofs, color proofs, photocopies, and sketches.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Casting ; Frizzer ; Il Lunedì della Repubblica ; Tempi Supplementari ; and Vomito . Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: Photographs and reportage published in Casting and Frizzer can also be found in Series V. Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Sparagna's personal correspondence unrelated to Frigidaire and other publications. Correspondents include political prisoners Renato Curcio and Oreste Scalzone and political journalist Luigi Pintor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographic prints, negatives, slides, and accompanying articles gathered for publication in Frigidaire , Frizzer , and other associated periodicals. Present are photographs from frequent contributors, submissions from readers, and images by Sparagna himself. Notable photographers present in this series include Mauro Baldrati, Letizia Battaglia, Pino Cimò, Antonio Carmelo Erotico, Toty Ruggieri, Riccardo Schicchi, Filippo Scòzzari, and Savik Shuster. Photographs encompass a range of subjects, including portraits of artists, authors, musicians, actors, and politicians from Italy and other countries; travel photography; political protests; art, music and theatre; and counterculture movements such as the punk movement. There are photographs documenting Sparagna's trip to Afghanistan during the Afghan-Soviet War, where he and Savik Shuster distributed fake issues of the Red Army newspaper Krasnai︠a︡ zvezda ( Red Star ) as part of "Operazione Chonkin." Other images document contributors' travels across the Americas, Africa, Asia, and Eastern Europe.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Photographer Files; Subject Files; and Reportage. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains original drawings, cartoons, comics, screen prints, sketches, photocopies, and collages by Sparagna and other artists, including Otto Gabos, Tanino Liberatore, Massimo Mattioli, José Muñoz, Giuseppe Palumbo, Andrea Pazienza, Filippo Scòzzari, Stefano Tamburini, and Gianfranco Vanni. Artwork in this series appears to be unrelated to any specific publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series documents Sparagna's involvement in Marxist and leftist political organizations during his student years in Naples and at the Università di Roma. Present are articles, interviews, assignments, and other writings by Sparagna throughout university and his early career as a journalist. There is also personal correspondence with other political activists from this period and subject files on Latin America.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Student Activism; Writings; Subject Files; and Other Papers. Within each subseries, materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.
